perturbation.original_text: If the Parties fail to settle the dispute amicably within sixty (60) Days of comme ncement of the negotiations, the dispute shall be settled through arbitration.
perturbation.changed_text: If the Parties fail to settle the dispute amicably within fifteen (15) Days of comme ncement of the negotiations, the dispute shall be settled through arbitration.
perturbation.explanation: Reducing the negotiation period to 15 days could be considered an unreasonably short timeframe to resolve complex international disputes, potentially conflicting with international arbitration norms that emphasize adequate opportunity for negotiation.
perturbation.law_explanation: Article 16 of the UNCITRAL Arbitration Rules emphasizes providing each party with a full opportunity to present their case. A 15-day negotiation period may be deemed insufficient to allow parties to fully explore settlement options before resorting to arbitration, potentially contravening the spirit of good faith negotiation inherent in the UNCITRAL rules.

perturbation.justification: The changed text reduces the negotiation period from 60 to 15 days. The Law Explanation states this "may be deemed insufficient to allow parties to fully explore settlement options before resorting to arbitration, potentially contravening the spirit of good faith negotiation inherent in the UNCITRAL rules." Snippet 2, referencing the "WIPO Arbitration Rules", discusses the "Conduct of the Arbitration" and "General Powers of the Tribunal". While it doesn't specify a minimum negotiation period, it suggests an expectation of thorough proceedings, including time for parties to present their case. Reducing the negotiation period to 15 days creates a contradiction as it potentially undermines the opportunity for parties to fully explore settlement options, conflicting with the expectation of good faith negotiation and the principles underlying arbitration rules.

perturbation.original_text: This Confidentiality Agreement shall enter into force on the date of the last signature by or on behalf of both Parties and shall remain in force for a pe riod of five (5) years, notwithstanding any earlier purported termination of this Confidenti ality Agreement for whatever reason.
perturbation.changed_text: This Confidentiality Agreement shall enter into force on the date of the last signature by or on behalf of both Parties and shall remain in force for a pe riod of one (1) year, notwithstanding any earlier purported termination of this Confidenti ality Agreement for whatever reason.
perturbation.explanation: Reducing the confidentiality period to one year may be insufficient to protect certain types of trade secrets or proprietary information, potentially conflicting with legal standards for trade secret protection.
perturbation.law_explanation: The UTSA defines trade secrets and provides legal protection for them as long as they remain confidential and provide a competitive advantage.  A one-year confidentiality period might be deemed insufficient to maintain the secrecy of certain trade secrets, thereby undermining the protections afforded by the UTSA.

perturbation.justification: The changed text reduces the confidentiality period from five years to one year. The Law Explanation states that a one-year confidentiality period might be deemed insufficient to maintain the secrecy of certain trade secrets, thereby undermining the protections afforded by the UTSA. This aligns with the Inconsistencies Legal Contradiction definition, which states that a legal contradiction arises when a contract sets a deadline or requirement that violates federal or state law, making the contractual terms unenforceable or illegal. Although there is no specific law snippet provided, the Law Explanation sufficiently describes the potential legal contradiction.
